plugin_desc: Fortinet-specific SNMP collection template for FortiGate firewalls. In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics and 64-bit HC traffic counters, it collects FORTINET-FORTIGATE-MIB private OIDs for device health (CPU utilization, memory utilization and capacity) and firewall-specific state (IPv4 active sessions, active IPsec VPN tunnels and HA mode). Supported brands/models — Fortinet FortiGate firewalls (FortiOS).
name: Firewall

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the overall CPU utilization of the FortiGate firewall (FORTINET-FORTIGATE-MIB fgSysCpuUsage). Sustained high CPU usage may indicate heavy traffic inspection load, attack activity or a control-plane bottleneck.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the overall memory utilization of the FortiGate firewall (FORTINET-FORTIGATE-MIB fgSysMemUsage, reported directly as a percentage). When memory pressure is high the device may enter conserve mode and drop new sessions.

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total physical memory capacity of the FortiGate firewall (FORTINET-FORTIGATE-MIB fgSysMemCapacity, reported in KB and converted to bytes).

metrics.name: firewall_active_sessions
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of active IPv4 sessions currently tracked by the FortiGate firewall (FORTINET-FORTIGATE-MIB fgSysSesCount). A rapidly rising session count can indicate a connection flood or session-table exhaustion risk.

metrics.name: firewall_vpn_tunnels
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of currently active IPsec VPN tunnels on the FortiGate firewall (FORTINET-FORTIGATE-MIB fgVpnTunnelUpCount). A sudden drop may indicate a VPN outage affecting site-to-site connectivity.

metrics.name: firewall_ha_mode
metrics.description: This metric reports the high-availability mode of the FortiGate firewall (FORTINET-FORTIGATE-MIB fgHaSystemMode): standalone, active-active or active-passive. It helps confirm whether the expected HA topology is in effect.

metrics.name: interface_ifAdminStatus
metrics.description: This metric indicates the administrative status of the network interface.

metrics.name: interface_ifOperStatus
metrics.description: This metric reflects the actual operational status of the network interface.

metrics.name: interface_ifSpeed
metrics.description: This metric indicates the maximum data transmission speed supported by the network interface.

metrics.name: interface_ifInErrors
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of incoming error packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifOutErrors
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of outgoing error packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifInDiscards
metrics.description: This metric represents the average rate of incoming discarded packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifOutDiscards
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of outgoing discarded packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifInUcastPkts
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of incoming unicast packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifOutUcastPkts
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of outgoing unicast packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCInOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCOutOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: device_total_incoming_traffic
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of total bytes received by the entire device over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: device_total_outgoing_traffic
metrics.description: This metric represents the average rate of total bytes sent by the entire device over the past 5 minutes.
